organization.organization_background: Utkrusht is a proof-of-skills marketplace that helps agile teams find talent with strong fundamental concepts with within days. We help aspiring professionals improve their profile with continuous improvement in their skills, documenting their journey and helping them fulfill their potential. We primarily build, conduct, proctor and disseminate proof-of-skill assessments in Technical domains like AI, ML, Fullstack development, High scale distributed systems, DevOps etc but do help non-tech roles like Sales, HR, Marketing etc

role_context: An advanced NodeJs engineer with 6+ years of experience is expected to independently design, build, and optimize production-grade backend services, APIs, and asynchronous workflows that support high-volume, business-critical applications. On a day-to-day basis, they own decisions around application structure, performance tuning, error handling, security, observability, and integration patterns, and are trusted to diagnose complex runtime, scalability, and concurrency issues without requiring close guidance from senior engineers. They are expected to make sound technical trade-offs across frameworks, libraries, event-driven patterns, data access approaches, and deployment environments, while ensuring codebases remain maintainable, testable, and resilient under changing requirements. In this role, they typically lead implementation of complex features and platform improvements end to end, seeking senior input mainly for broader architectural direction, cross-system dependencies, or especially high-risk design decisions.
questions_prompt:
Please ensure the questions you ask cover the key functional, logical, architectural, implementation, debugging, optimization, review, and problem-solving areas from the competency scope for {name}, matched to {proficiency} proficiency ({yoe} years experience).

Relevant competency topics and sub-skills to cover from the provided scope: no explicit topic areas were provided in the competency long scope for NodeJs, so focus on practical NodeJs work-item scenarios that assess real-world backend engineering ability rather than syntax, framework setup, testing tools, or installation/configuration knowledge.

This is a basic proficiency, so the tasks need to match one year of experience only. Somebody with one year of experience does not have extensive production experience and their concepts are usually shaky, so the complexity of the task needs to be basic only. Let the candidate be able to finish this within 15 minutes.

Prefer tasks that assess: understanding of requirements and constraints; reasoning about ambiguous real-world situations; quality of technical decisions and tradeoff analysis; identifying root causes, risks, and edge cases; proposing or implementing pragmatic solutions; and reviewing or improving existing work.

The assessment should use realistic scenarios such as building or fixing a small NodeJs backend feature, debugging a runtime or async-flow issue, reviewing an API or service design, improving error handling or input validation, analyzing performance or reliability concerns in a simple service, reasoning about module structure or separation of concerns, or making safe changes to existing server-side code. Avoid trivia, trick questions, pure syntax recall, framework-specific setup steps, tool installation, test runner configuration, or semantics-only questions.

The goal is to evaluate applied competence, practical problem-solving, implementation and review ability, sound technical judgment, and the candidate's ability to deliver realistic solutions within the expected time constraints for their proficiency level.
